sections.search-results:
Zhuge Dan’s Rebellion- Right or Wrong?

	Date: 03/2007
	Replies: 7
sections.biography-source-undefined:
Zhuge Dan, styled Gongxiu, was from Yangdu county, Langya commandery, and a descendant of Zhuge Feng. There is nothing yet known of his life before he became a Gentleman of the Secretariat, which occurred before 224.

In 224, When Zhuge Dan was Gentleman of the Secretariat, he and Du Ji crossed the Tao river, when a fierce wind overturned the boat and cast Zhuge Dan and Du Ji into the water. Some brave men swam across the river to save Zhuge Dan but the latter said, “Save Marquis Du first!” While Zhuge Dan was barely saved, Du Ji drowned. Zhuge Dan, being Gentleman of the Secretariat, was made Prefect of Xingyang.

Zhuge Dan held the title of Imperial Censor who Assists the Secretariat (御史中丞尚書). Zhuge Dan was not favored by Cao Rui, the official reason being that he associated heavily with Deng Yang and his friends, whom Cao Rui detested for their superficial and flamboyant manners. He was actually removed from his title, but was restored during the Zhengshi era (240–249) due to the efforts of Xiahou Xuan. He later left Luoyang to serve as Inspector of Yang Province, then General who Illuminates Might (昭武將軍).

Sima Zhao once tried to gain favor with Zhuge Dan by sending Jia Chong to award him, but Zhuge Dan was hostile. According to the 魏末傳, Jia Chong told Zhuge Dan, “The Luo region is full of talents, all of whom look forward to the abdication and replacement [of Wei]. You are aware of that. What do you make of it?” Zhuge Dan, with a stern countenance, said, “Are you not the son of [Inspector] Jia [Kui] of Yu Province? You received the grace of Wei for generations, but how can you betray our empire so by want of the Wei dynasty being given to others? This is not something I can bear to hear. Should the Luo region have trouble, I will die for it.” Jia Chong did not reply to that remark. The imperial court, probably under the machinations of Sima Zhao, suspected of Zhuge Dan of harboring intentions to rebel, and had an edict making him Excellency of Works to see if he would consent to being summoned back to Luoyang. Their suspicions were true, for Zhuge Dan, incensed by the edict, rebelled in Yang province, which began around the Yihai day of the 5th lunar month of 257. He first called up his generals to attack the Inspector, who was Yue Chen, and successfully killed him. Zhuge Dan had his base of operations in Shouchun, adopting a defensive posture.

He tasked his Chief Clerk 長史, a certain Wu Gang, to deliver his young son, Zhuge Jing to Wu in an attempt to get its support, and the one in charge of its government, Sun Chen, agreed and sent a sizable army to Zhuge Dan. Wu even granted Zhuge Dan many titles such as Governor of Qing Province and Marquis of Shouchun. Wei, however, sent the General who Garrisons the South, Wang Ji, to siege Shouchun, preventing the majority of Wu troops to expand the numbers of the rebellion.

The situation in Shouchun turned worse for Zhuge Dan. In the 1st lunar month of 258, Sima Zhao reinforced the sieging army. Their army beat drums, shouted, and scaled the city walls, terrifying its inhabitants. Zhuge Dan felt trapped and desperate, so he rode a horse by himself and led his direct subordinates to rush out of the small gates of the city. The troops of Hu Fen met them in battle and was able to kill Zhuge Dan. They sent his head to Luoyang while his clan was exterminated to the third degree, at least in Wei. Some of the direct subordinates who rode out with Zhuge Dan were executed for their refusal to surrender. It is said they all cried out, “We die for Lord Zhuge! We have no regrets!” Many people sympathized with them. Thus, the rebellion was destroyed after lasting slightly over a year.

full_text:
Zhuge Dan (Gongxiu) 諸葛誕 (公休)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Zhuge Dan (諸葛誕), styledGongxiu (公休), was born in Yangdu county, Langya commandery. Tried to rebel against Sima Zhao, but was executed. Zhuge Dan was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom. Zhuge Dan lived during the Three Kingdoms period of Chinese history.
